title: Medium 1
text: The cat sat on the mat. The dog ran fast.

title: Medium 2
text: Where is the big red ball? Can you see it near the tree?

title: Medium 3
text: We bought apples, bananas, oranges, grapes, and pears.

title: Medium 4
text: I can't find my book. Don't worry, it's on the desk.

title: Medium 5
text: The dog wagged its tail. It's very happy today.

title: Medium 6
text: There are 7 days in a week and 12 months in a year.

title: Medium 7
text: I believe the calendar shows February and accident happened on Tuesday.

title: Medium 8
text: Please write your address and answer the question carefully.

title: Medium 9
text: We went to the library to learn about history and natural science.

title: Medium 10
text: My favourite animal is an elephant because it is huge and clever.

title: Medium 11
text: Plants need light, water, air, nutrients, and space to grow tall.

title: Medium 12
text: The River Thames flows through London, the capital of England.

title: Medium 13
text: Ben Nevis is in Scotland, Snowdon is in Wales, and Scafell Pike is in England.

title: Medium 14
text: Half of 20 is 10, and a quarter of 12 is 3. Can you work it out?

title: Medium 15
text: Quarter past three is 3:15. Half past six is 6:30. What time is it now?

title: Medium 16
text: Granite is hard, chalk is soft, and sandstone lets water through.

title: Medium 17
text: We see because light reflects off objects into our eyes. Darkness is absence of light.

title: Medium 18
text: Friction slows things down. Ice has low friction, carpet has high friction.

title: Medium 19
text: Iron, nickel, cobalt, and steel are magnetic. Copper and aluminium are not.

title: Medium 20
text: Eat a balanced diet with carbohydrates, protein, fruit and vegetables, dairy, and healthy fats.

title: Medium 21
text: Our skeleton supports us, protects organs, and helps us move with muscles.

title: Medium 22
text: Paris is the capital of France, Berlin is the capital of Germany, Rome is the capital of Italy.

title: Medium 23
text: Never share your password or address online. Tell a trusted adult if worried.

title: Medium 24
text: I won't forget my coat because it might rain later today.

title: Medium 25
text: Who, what, where, when, why, and how are question words we use every day?

title: Hard 1
text: "Look out!" shouted Sam. "The ball is coming towards us fast!"

title: Hard 2
text: Wow! What an amazing rainbow after the heavy rain storm!

title: Hard 3
text: London, Edinburgh, Cardiff, and Belfast are capitals of the four countries in the United Kingdom.

title: Hard 4
text: You need three things for plants: light, water, and air plus nutrients and space.

title: Hard 5
text: Ben Nevis (1,345 metres high) is the tallest mountain in the United Kingdom.

title: Hard 6
text: One half equals 50 percent, one quarter equals 25 percent, and three quarters equals 75 percent.

title: Hard 7
text: 14:25 in 24-hour time is 2:25 pm. Midnight is 00:00 and noon is 12:00.

title: Hard 8
text: Roman numerals: I is 1, V is 5, X is 10, L is 50. Clock faces use I to XII.

title: Hard 9
text: A rectangle 7 cm long and 3 cm wide has perimeter 20 cm because 7+3+7+3 equals 20.

title: Hard 10
text: There are 100 centimetres in a metre, 1000 millimetres in a metre, and 1000 grams in a kilogram.

title: Hard 11
text: Photosynthesis: carbon dioxide plus water plus light makes glucose plus oxygen in leaves.

title: Hard 12
text: Seeds travel by wind (dandelion), animal hooks (burr), water (coconut), and explosion (pea pod).

title: Hard 13
text: Sedimentary rocks form from layers squashed, igneous from cooled magma, metamorphic changed by heat and pressure.

title: Hard 14
text: Light travels in straight lines. Shadows form where light is blocked. Mirror angle in equals angle out.

title: Hard 15
text: Friction opposes motion. Air resistance is friction with air. Parachutes increase air resistance to slow fall.

title: Hard 16
text: Magnets have north and south poles. Like poles repel, opposite poles attract. Magnetic field works at a distance.

title: Hard 17
text: An algorithm is precise step-by-step instructions. Debugging means finding and fixing errors.

title: Hard 18
text: The Pyrenees separate France and Spain. The Alps run through France, Switzerland, Italy, Austria, and more.

title: Hard 19
text: Occasionally, separate, definite, accommodation, and embarrass are tricky Year 3 and 4 words to practise.

title: Hard 20
text: The girl's book means one girl owns it. The girls' books means many girls own them.

title: Hard 21
text: A strong password is long with mix of letters, numbers, and symbols. Never share personal information online.

title: Hard 22
text: Evaporation turns liquid to gas, condensation turns gas to droplets forming clouds, precipitation falls as rain.

title: Hard 23
text: Muscles work in pairs to pull bones. Tendons attach muscle to bone. The skeleton protects brain, heart, lungs.

title: Hard 24
text: Three eighths of forty is fifteen because divide by eight then times three. Equivalent fractions have same value.

title: Hard 25
text: "Well done!" cheered the teacher. "You typed 75 lessons with capitals, commas, apostrophes, quotes, numbers, and brackets!"
